title: 5D: Multi-Host Buyer-Seller Agent Integration Test
description: Deploy seller agent on one real host and buyer agent on a separate real host. Test full agentic deal negotiation flow between them over the network: buyer discovers seller inventory, initiates deal request, negotiates terms, books deal, and verifies deal appears on both sides. Validate MCP server communication, authentication, and end-to-end deal lifecycle across hosts.
close_reason: Deferred to post-v2 operational phase — requires multi-host deployment infrastructure

title: 1A-Phase3: FreeWheel PG Cross-MCP Booking + Reporting
description: Full Programmatic Guaranteed workflow spanning both MCPs. Streaming Hub: IO + campaign + placement + deal setup (steps 1-6). Buyer Cloud: campaign creation and binding via shared deal_id (steps 7-10). BC reporting via v2_get_reporting_*, check_campaign_health, deal_analyst. BC creative management via create_creative, v2_attach_creative_to_line_item. Cross-MCP binding via external deal_id as shared key. Partial failure handling: SH success + BC failure returns binding_status=partial with warning. Deliverable: complete PG workflow with campaign execution and reporting. TENTATIVE: Awaiting FreeWheel/NBCU review of dual-MCP proposal. Key open questions: (1) How does BC campaign reference SH deal by deal_id? (2) Auth scope across both MCPs? (3) Deal creation sequencing constraints?
close_reason: Replaced by seller-mwy (FreeWheel PG Cross-MCP Campaign Management)

title: 2H: Index Exchange REST SSP Integration
description: Index Exchange SSP client using their REST API at api.indexexchange.com. Subclass of RESTSSPClient with IX-specific API paths, field names, and response parsing. Deal CRUD + troubleshooting stub (IX has no dedicated troubleshooting endpoint). Docs: kb.indexexchange.com/publishers/pmp/deals_api.htm
close_reason: IndexExchangeSSPClient with IX-specific API paths, field names, response parsing

title: 3G: Infrastructure-as-Code Deployment (CloudFormation/Terraform)
description: Modular IaC deployment supporting both AWS CloudFormation and Terraform. Single codebase with provider abstraction — user picks their tool. Covers: VPC/networking, ECS Fargate or Lambda for the seller agent, Redis (ElastiCache) for prod storage, SQS queues for event bus subscribers, API Gateway or ALB for the FastAPI endpoints, IAM roles, secrets management (SSM Parameter Store), CloudWatch logging. Structured as reusable modules: networking, compute, storage, messaging, api-gateway. Terraform modules and CloudFormation nested stacks follow the same logical structure so either can be used. Creates infra/ directory with terraform/ and cloudformation/ subdirectories. Phase 3 — post-pilot.
notes:
Planning complete. Scope agreed with user:

**Storage Architecture:** Hybrid Postgres + Redis
- PostgreSQL (Aurora Serverless v2) for business data (inventory, orders, negotiations, audit)
- Redis (ElastiCache) for sessions, caching, event pub/sub
- New PostgresBackend + HybridStorageBackend implementations needed

**IaC Targets:** Both CloudFormation + Terraform, each with Docker/ECS
- Dockerfile (multi-stage) + docker-compose.yml for local dev
- AWS CloudFormation: nested stacks (networking, compute/ECS Fargate, storage/Aurora+ElastiCache, ALB, SSM secrets, CloudWatch)
- Terraform: modular structure mirroring CF, AWS-first but extensible to GCP/Azure

**Implementation Order:**
1. Dockerfile + docker-compose
2. PostgreSQL storage backend (JSONB KV pattern, drop-in compatible)
3. Hybrid storage router (routes key prefixes to Postgres vs Redis)
4. CloudFormation templates
5. Terraform modules
6. Docs and config updates

**Key Decisions:**
- JSONB KV pattern for Postgres (same interface as SQLite/Redis, no schema migration)
- Aurora Serverless v2 (scales to zero, no capacity planning)
- ECS Fargate (serverless containers)
- SSM Parameter Store for secrets
- Modular IaC (each concern is a separate template/module)
close_reason: Closed

title: 4C: Template-Based Deal Creation Endpoint
description: Add POST /api/v1/deals/from-template endpoint and create_deal_from_template MCP tool. Accepts structured template params (inventory_type, targeting, deal_type, impressions, max_cpm, flight dates, buyer_context) instead of requiring a pre-existing proposal. Reuses DealRequestFlow internally with structured params.
close_reason: Closed

title: 3B: MCP Server + Claude Desktop / ChatGPT Integration
description: Real MCP SSE server exposing 62 tools for Claude Desktop and ChatGPT. Includes interactive setup wizard (developer phase in Claude Code, business phase in Claude Desktop), all deal/inventory/pricing/approval/buyer/curator operations. Replaces static builder guides with conversational setup.
close_reason: MCP server with 62 tools, setup wizard, Claude Desktop + ChatGPT guides, developer setup guide

title: 1H: Media Kit & Public Inventory Discovery
description: Public-facing inventory catalog for buyer agents (authenticated or not). Three-layer package management: Layer 1 - sync packages from FreeWheel/GAM/SSP (admin sees what exists). Layer 2 - seller curation and promotion with wizard to create new package iterations (e.g. Winter Olympics Package, Peacock Premium CTV Bundle). Layer 3 - agent-assembled dynamic packages from existing placements/deal IDs. Public endpoint (no auth) returns packages, pricing ranges, demographics. Authenticated endpoint adds exact pricing, availability, performance. Natural language search + structured browsing. Creates src/ad_seller/models/media_kit.py. Modifies api/main.py, discovery_inquiry_flow.py. Depends on 1D for Layer 1 ad server sync.
close_reason: Closed

title: 3D: Scheduled Periodic Inventory Sync
description: Add configurable interval-based automatic inventory sync from ad server. Currently sync is manual/on-demand only. Should support configurable cron-like intervals so publishers don't need to manually trigger syncs. Referenced in docs/guides/inventory-sync.md planned improvements.
close_reason: Closed

title: 4K: Deal Export Formats for DSP Connectors
description: Add GET /api/v1/deals/export endpoint with format parameter (ttd, dv360, amazon, xandr). Returns deal data formatted for each DSP's import requirements. Enables buyer Phase 4D platform connectors to pull deals in native format.
close_reason: Closed

title: 4G: Curator Support
description: Add curator support per OpenDirect 3.0 spec. Curator role packages/selects inventory they don't own, applies audience or content intent. Add curator_id field in supply chain responses, support curator-packaged deals in template endpoint. Depends on IAB Deals API v1.0 integration (seller-a3k).
close_reason: Curator models, registry with Agent Range day-one, curated deal endpoints, schain with curator node

title: 2A-2: Order Audit & Reporting
description: Add admin-facing audit and reporting endpoints for order lifecycle. GET /api/v1/orders/report (summary: counts by status, avg time-in-state, transition frequency). GET /api/v1/orders/{order_id}/audit (detailed audit log with actor, timestamps, reasons, metadata for every transition). Support date range filters, status filters, and actor filters. Enables ops admins on seller side to audit order history and generate compliance reports.
close_reason: Audit + report endpoints with filters, 9 tests

title: 1J: Agent Registry Integration & Discoverability
description: Integrate with IAB Tech Lab Agent Registry (AAMP) to discover registered buyer/seller agents. Replace hardcoded SELLER_ENDPOINTS with registry-based discovery. Add human-in-the-loop approval gate for non-registered agents. Must be completed before buyer agent auth (1I).
close_reason: Agent registry integration complete: models, AAMP client, registry service, API endpoints, blocked enforcement, 68 unit tests, 235 total passing

title: 1A-Phase2: FreeWheel PD/PA Deal Booking (Streaming Hub Only)
description: Create and activate Preferred Deal and Private Auction deals via Streaming Hub MCP only. create_deal() via pub_1_0_createdeal + pub_1_0_activatedeal, update_deal() via pub_1_0_updatedeal, IO management via pub_1_1_create-an-insertion-order + pub_1_1_book-an-insertion-order, line items via pub_1_1_create-a-campaign + pub_1_1_create-a-placement. Deliverable: full PD/PA deal lifecycle — buyer gets deal_id to enter in any DSP. TENTATIVE: Awaiting FreeWheel/NBCU review of dual-MCP proposal.
close_reason: book_deal() implemented in adapter with SH+BC support

title: 2G: SSP Deal Distribution in ExecutionActivationFlow
description: Wire SSPRegistry into ExecutionActivationFlow so deals created by the seller agent are automatically distributed to configured SSPs. When a deal is booked, push it to the appropriate SSP(s) based on routing rules. SSP handles DSP-side distribution.

title: 4A: Rename NonAgenticDSPFlow to DealRequestFlow
description: Rename NonAgenticDSPFlow to DealRequestFlow across 6 files. The old name is pejorative — implies DSPs are non-agentic. New name is neutral. Files: flows/non_agentic_dsp_flow.py → deal_request_flow.py, flows/__init__.py, interfaces/chat/main.py, interfaces/cli/main.py, examples/non_agentic_dsp.py → deal_request_example.py. Add backward-compat alias in __init__.py.

Include dummy FreeWheel responses, dummy SSP responses (PubMatic, Index Exchange), dummy inventory data, and dummy rate cards. Validate full workflow without real external services.

Builds on counter.py, yield_optimizer.py, pricing_rules_engine.py. Depends on 1F (sessions), 1H (packages).

title: Pickup: Deal Library session context 2026-03-11
description:
SESSION PICKUP CONTEXT — 2026-03-11

## What happened this session

1. Created 4 Inventory Sync Enhancement issues (seller Phase 4: 4A-4D) from planned improvements in docs/guides/inventory-sync.md
2. Closed seller-ryn (3C: Deployment & Operations Guide) and seller-299 (3G: IaC Deployment) which were completed in prior session
3. Designed the Deal Jockey Sub-Agent epic with Aidan input (see /Users/bjt/Documents/crewAITechLabAgent/deal-jockey-planning-context.md)
4. Created 8 seller Deal Jockey issues (Phase 5: 5A-5H) with dependencies
5. Created 18 buyer Deal Jockey issues (Phase 5: 5A-5R) with dependencies
6. Updated generate_progress.py in both repos with new phase maps
7. Synced and pushed both repos to GitHub

## Key design decisions (see deal-jockey-planning-context.md + plan at ~/.claude/plans/noble-frolicking-hennessy.md)

- Deal Jockey = renamed/expanded DSP Specialist (buyer Level 2 agent)
- NonAgenticDSPFlow → DealRequestFlow (seller rename, backward-compat alias)
- IAB Deals API is the DSP abstraction — no per-DSP adapters
- Plugin interface (DealReplacementStrategy + SupplyPathScorer protocols) for external optimization providers like Agent Range
- Rules-based defaults ship out of the box; ML optimization is opt-in via plugin
- Deal ID lineage tracked via parent_deal_id FK
- Supply path scoring: 4 weights (transparency, fee, trust, performance)

## Buyer re-phasing status

- Buyer has 8 orphaned "Other" issues (state machine, event bus, docs) that need re-phasing into Phase 4
- Brian decided Aidan will handle buyer re-phasing — DO NOT re-phase buyer issues
- Seller phasing is clean (Phases 1-5, no gaps, no orphans)

## What's next

- seller-a3k (IAB Deals API v1.0) is in_progress — depends on seller-dcd (FreeWheel)
- Deal Jockey Phase 5 implementation can start with foundation tasks that have no blockers:
  - Seller: seller-6g3 (rename), seller-a5b (supply chain endpoint), seller-wgd (performance endpoint), seller-gj1 (bulk deals)
  - Buyer: buyer-e6a (rename), buyer-1q3 (rename), buyer-rsf (schema migration), buyer-91l (event types), buyer-ez3 (push receiver)
- Aidan will re-phase buyer "Other" issues into Phase 4
close_reason: Closed

Deal push endpoint (HTTP POST to DSP), deal status query (poll buyer for BuyerSeat status: pending/approved/rejected/ready_to_serve/active/complete), full Deal object spec (terms, inventory, curation), seller status management (active/paused/pending/archived). Maps our DealOutput model to IAB Deal object. Depends on 2A (state machine), 1A (FreeWheel).

Returns sellers.json-like self-description: seller_id, seller_name, seller_type (PUBLISHER/INTERMEDIARY/BOTH), is_direct, domain, supported_deal_types, schain nodes. Optional .well-known/agent.json for agent discovery (AdCP pattern). Lightweight — not full OAuth (that is Phase 3). Creates src/ad_seller/auth/ (api_key_auth.py, agent_registry.py). Modifies api/main.py, settings.py. No blockers.

Configurable transitions with guard conditions. Audit log of all state transitions with timestamps and actor info. Unify ExecutionStatus (flow_state.py:19-34) with ExecutionOrderStatus (core.py:125-132). Depends on 1G (event bus for approval states).

Enables buyer agents and human admins to check order status at any time.

Parallels GAM dual-client pattern (SOAP+REST). SH handles: inventory, IOs, campaigns, placements, deals, audiences, forecasting. BC handles: campaign execution, creatives, bidding, reporting. Cross-MCP binding via shared deal_id for PG bookings. Proposal doc: docs/FREEWHEEL_MCP_TOOL_PROPOSAL.md. Implementation plan: see plan file. description: Abstract AdServerClient interface that GAM and FreeWheel both implement. Factory selects based on config. Defines the contract FreeWheel must satisfy regardless of API or MCP protocol. Extract interface from existing GAM client. Creates src/ad_seller/clients/ad_server_base.py. Modifies execution_activation_flow.py to replace if/elif branching with polymorphic dispatch. No blockers — start immediately.
close_reason: Implemented ad server abstraction layer: AdServerClient abstract interface with normalized models (AdServerOrder, AdServerLineItem, AdServerDeal, etc.), GAMAdServerClient adapter wrapping existing SOAP+REST clients, factory function get_ad_server_client(), and updated ExecutionActivationFlow to use polymorphic dispatch.

description: Add change detection to inventory sync so only modified/new/deleted items are processed instead of full re-sync. Reduces sync time and ad server API load for large inventories. Referenced in docs/guides/inventory-sync.md planned improvements.

title: 1G: Human-in-the-Loop Event Bus
description: Reusable async pub/sub messaging system for human-in-the-loop tasks throughout the workflow. NOT just approval hooks — a general-purpose event bus that developers can connect to webhooks, SQS queues, CRM systems, Slack, or any external system. Supports: publish events (approval needed, review needed, finance sign-off), subscribe to events (webhook, SQS, pubsubhubbub), respond to events (approve/reject/comment via API). Use cases: deal approval above threshold, creative review, finance budget approval, ad ops team review, any async human task in the adops workflow. Creates src/ad_seller/events/ (event_bus.py, subscribers.py, webhook_handler.py). Modifies proposal_handling_flow.py, deal_generation_flow.py, api/main.py. No blockers.
close_reason: Implemented event bus with InMemoryEventBus (dev) and StorageEventBus (SQLite/Redis), ApprovalGate for human-in-the-loop approval, event emission in proposal/deal/execution flows, 6 new API endpoints for events and approvals, and configurable approval gates behind feature flags.

description: Post-deal modifications: flight date changes, impression adjustments, price mods, creative swaps. Change request model with diff tracking (what changed, who, when). Re-validation pipeline triggering pricing/availability/approval checks. Ad server update propagation to FreeWheel/GAM. Maps to PRD UpdateCampaign, CreateNewProduct, ReviseCreative. Depends on 2A (state machine).

title: 3B: Builder Guides for Vertical Customization
description:
# 3B: Builder Guides for Vertical Customization

## Overview
Scenario-driven guides for teams customizing the seller agent for their specific business. Each guide walks through a real vertical with concrete examples of what to change and where.

## Problem
The platform is highly configurable but it's not obvious how to:
- Customize pricing models and discount tiers
- Update prompts in sub-agents for domain-specific negotiation
- Add business-specific "skills" (tools) to agents
- Enforce compliance policies (HIPAA, political ad rules, etc.)
- Configure inventory types and deal structures for different media

## Planned Guides

### Guide 1: Healthcare Publisher (e.g., WebMD)
- HIPAA privacy skills that prevent prohibited targeting
- Buyer vetting and restricted advertiser categories
- Custom pricing for pharma vs. general advertisers
- Compliance agent that validates proposals against regulations

### Guide 2: CTV/Streaming Publisher (e.g., political ad rules)
- Political advertising policies and seasonal overrides
- Linear + CTV unified inventory setup
- FreeWheel integration specifics
- Content adjacency and brand safety rules

### Guide 3: News/Media Publisher (general web)
- High-volume programmatic with floor price management
- Category blocking and sensitive content policies
- Header bidding integration alongside direct deals
- Seasonal pricing (elections, holidays, tentpole events)

### Guide 4: Custom Pricing & Negotiation
- How to modify pricing rules engine tiers and volume curves
- Updating negotiation agent prompts for different strategies
- Adding custom tools (e.g., competitive rate lookup, audience valuation)
- Configuring approval gates for high-value deals

## Each Guide Covers
1. **Business context** — who this is for, what makes them different
2. **Configuration changes** — settings.py, pricing tiers, feature flags
3. **Agent customization** — prompt modifications, new tools, skill additions
4. **Policy enforcement** — how to add compliance rules that can't be overridden
5. **Testing** — how to validate customizations work correctly

## Timing
Best written after Phase 1 core is stable. Depends on having the full feature set available so guides aren't speculative.
close_reason: Replaced by MCP server + interactive setup wizard (3B becomes the guide)

title: 1L: FreeWheel Integration Testing & Validation
description: End-to-end testing against live FreeWheel MCP endpoints. Validate: list_inventory() normalization against real responses, book_deal() round-trip, inventory_mode deals_only vs full, error handling for MCP disconnects, sample response fixtures for unit tests.
close_reason: Deferred to post-v2 — requires live FreeWheel MCP endpoints

title: 4J: Deal Deprecation API
description: Add POST /api/v1/deals/{id}/deprecate endpoint. Marks a deal as deprecated with reason and optional replacement_deal_id. Returns deprecation confirmation. Enables buyer Deal Jockey to sunset underperforming deals. Maps to buyer-te6b.2.4 (deal deprecation analysis).
close_reason: POST /api/v1/deals/{id}/deprecate + GET /api/v1/deals/{id}/lineage — deprecation with reason/replacement + lineage chain walker

title: 5C: Seller Agent Tests with Real Data
description: Create integration tests that hit real external services (FreeWheel Streaming Hub, PubMatic MCP, Index Exchange API) with test/sandbox credentials. Validate real inventory sync, real deal booking, real SSP deal distribution. Requires sandbox accounts and API keys configured.
close_reason: Deferred to post-v2 operational phase — requires sandbox credentials for FreeWheel, PubMatic, IX

title: 3C: Deployment & Operations Guide
description:
# 3C: Deployment & Operations Guide

## Overview
Production deployment and day-2 operations documentation. Pairs with 3G (IaC) to provide both the automation and the human-readable runbook.

## Sections
1. **Quickstart** — Local development setup, env vars, first API call
2. **Deployment Options** — Docker, AWS (CloudFormation), Terraform, bare metal
3. **Storage Configuration** — SQLite (dev) vs Redis (prod), migration path
4. **Monitoring & Observability** — Event bus as audit log, key metrics, alerting
5. **Scaling** — When to move from SQLite to Redis, horizontal scaling considerations
6. **Security** — API authentication, buyer identity verification, secrets management
7. **Troubleshooting** — Common issues, event bus diagnostics, approval timeout handling

## Dependencies
- Benefits from 3G (IaC) being complete for deployment automation sections
- Benefits from 3A (API Reference) for cross-linking
close_reason: Closed

title: 4I: Deal Migration Support Endpoint
description: Add POST /api/v1/deals/{id}/migrate endpoint. Allows buyer Deal Jockey to request deal replacement — seller validates the new terms, creates a replacement deal with parent_deal_id lineage, and marks the old deal as deprecated. Maps to buyer-te6b.2.11 (Deal migration tool).
close_reason: POST /api/v1/deals/{id}/migrate — creates replacement deal with parent_deal_id lineage, auto-deprecates old deal

title: 3A: API & Architecture Reference Documentation
description:
# 3A: API & Architecture Reference Documentation

## Overview
Living documentation that gets updated incrementally as each feature lands. This is the technical reference for developers integrating with or extending the seller agent.

## Sections
1. **Architecture Overview** — System diagram, flow lifecycle, agent topology
2. **REST API Reference** — All endpoints (products, pricing, proposals, deals, events, approvals), request/response schemas, error codes
3. **Configuration Reference** — All settings.py options with defaults and env var names
4. **Storage Schema** — Key patterns, data models, backend options (SQLite/Redis)
5. **Event Bus & Approval Gates** — Event types, approval workflow, resume pattern
6. **Agent & Crew Reference** — Each CrewAI agent's role, tools, and prompt structure
7. **Extension Points** — Where to add custom tools, agents, pricing rules, ad server adapters

## Approach
- Created as a docs/ directory with markdown files
- Updated incrementally as each Phase 1/2 feature lands
- Each feature PR should include a docs update (even if just a new endpoint added to the API ref)

## Deliverables
- docs/architecture.md
- docs/api-reference.md
- docs/configuration.md
- docs/storage.md
- docs/events-and-approvals.md
- docs/agents.md
- docs/extending.md
close_reason: Complete: 25+ seller doc pages, 15+ buyer doc pages, media kit docs, cross-linked GitHub Pages sites deployed

title: 1F: Multi-turn Session Persistence
description: Persist conversation state across hours/days so buyer agents can resume negotiations. Session model with session_id, conversation_history, current_proposal, negotiation_state. Currently chat history is in-memory only. Modifies chat/main.py, storage/base.py, api/main.py. Creates src/ad_seller/models/session.py. No blockers.
close_reason: Closed

title: 4D: Deal Performance Data Endpoint
description: Add GET /api/v1/deals/{id}/performance endpoint and get_deal_performance MCP tool. Returns delivery stats: impressions_available, impressions_served, fill_rate, win_rate, avg_cpm_actual, delivery_pacing. Placeholder/mock stats initially — real ad server integration later. Enables buyer SPO feedback loop.
close_reason: Closed

title: 1E: Linear TV Specialist Agent
description: Full linear TV channel specialist for NBCU. Dayparts, scatter/upfront deals, commercial pod positions, GRP-based buying, audience guarantees. Products: NBC Primetime :30, Bravo Reality :15, Telemundo :30. Creates linear_tv_inventory_agent.py. Modifies flow_state.py (add linear_tv type), product_setup_flow.py (NBCU products), inventory_crews.py. Maps to PRD NBC inputs: GetProducts, GetTargeting, GetRate, GetAvails. No blockers.
close_reason: Implemented full Linear TV Specialist Agent: 5 Pydantic models (Daypart, MakegoodTerms, SupplyPoolEntry, LinearTVProduct, LinearDeal), 12 tools (pricing/avails/forecasting/traffic), Level 2 agent with institutional knowledge backstory, crew integration, PublisherCrew routing, 6 default products (3 NBCU direct + 2 Comcast MVPD + 1 reseller), 210 DMA codes, 58 unit tests, Rivian R2 cross-platform example. Standards: TIP v5.1.0, OpenRTB 2.6, IAB sellers.json.

title: 2E: SSP Connector Abstraction (SSPClient ABC + Registry)
description: New SSPClient ABC with pluggable implementations for MCP-based SSPs (PubMatic) and REST-based SSPs (Magnite, Index Exchange). SSPRegistry supports multiple simultaneous SSPs with routing rules (e.g., ctv→pubmatic, display→magnite). Includes MCPSSPClient, RESTSSPClient, ssp_factory, and config.
close_reason: SSPClient ABC, MCPSSPClient, RESTSSPClient, SSPRegistry, ssp_factory implemented
